name: CREAMY KORMA
description: Korma doesn't have to contain a ridiculous amount of curry powder. That's because it's downright delicious without being hot. A creamy korma is a dish that's close to our hearts because it's mild and child-friendly, that special, but korma that I'd say anyone can cook like our family. Feel free to use any combination of vegetables you like. Serve with rice (see page 136) or Naan (see page 201).
ingredients: red bunch of fresh coriander, to serve
method: Preheat oven to 180°C | Line a baking tray | Liquidiser | Large frying pan

Peel the sweet potato, squash and carrots. Cut into 3cm chunks and arrange on the lined baking tray, brush with 1 tablespoon of the oil and season lightly with salt. Put the tray in the oven and roast for 30 minutes, turning the tray halfway through so that the veg gets evenly cooked
Put the cardamom pods with the end of a knife and tear them open. Tip the seeds into the liquidiser and discard the pods. Add the cloves and put on a medium-high heat. Toast for 2 minutes

Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add all the remaining 2 tablespoons oil and add the onions, bay leaf, cardamom and 5mg of the blanched almonds. Stir and cook for 10-12 minutes until soft. Add the garlic, ginger and chillies and cook for a further 2 minutes

Peel the ginger by scraping off the skin with a spoon, then roughly chop. Rip the stems from the chillies, roughly chop them (leaving in some of the seeds if you prefer a milder sauce, then finely chop. Peel and crush the garlic using the flat side of a knife. Add the ginger, garlic and half the turmeric to the pan. Cook for another 2 minutes

Take off the heat and leave everything to cool down. Remove the bay leaf. Transfer to the liquidiser with 200ml coconut milk and whizz to a smooth paste, about 60 seconds. Pour back into the pan and put on a medium heat

Add the roasted vegetables to the pan. Pour in the rest of the coconut milk and stir gently until well mixed. Reduce the heat and simmer for about 3 minutes. Add a splash of water if the sauce is too thick
Cut the limes in half and squeeze the juice over the curry, catching any pips in your other hand. Season to taste with salt

Finely chop the leaves from most of the coriander, putting the stalks from the coriander and save for another recipe, then chop the leaves and mix them into the curry. Garnish with any spring and slice the spring onions. Scatter a little over each portion
